topic: §4.2.2 post-EXP-267 adversarial review (Chapman/Sq misattribution fix + Sq-residual fourth horn)
our_argument_summary: EXP-267 patched §4.2.2 to address the c2 holes: (H1 MAJOR) the inherited "no physical mechanism" misattribution paragraph was replaced with a structural critique that quotes the dome's actual Tier 1 disclosure ("0.0 nT systematic (or ionospheric Sq disruption — competing mechanism exists, see WIN-068 honest caveat)") and pivots to the cite-Chapman-in-footnote-but-write-rule-as-if-Chapman-doesn't-exist structural argument; (H2 MODERATE) a new fourth-horn paragraph addresses the post-Sq-residual defense by demanding a pre-registered Sq-subtraction methodology anchored before the August 2026 measurement; (minor 4a) the §4.2.7 forward-link parenthetical was hoisted to its own paragraph. Date stamp updated 2026-04-26 → 2026-04-27. The c2 minor 3 (TLDR phrasing) and c2 minor 4 (E001–E009 stronger_arguments) were deliberately not addressed (analyst flagged optional). Verdict (Refuted by Data) holds; this c3 attacks the integrated text with attention to surviving holes the c2 review couldn't see post-integration.

live_quote_eclipse_falsification_rule.live_form: Eclipse 2026 — Fails if measured anomaly is within ±3 nT of 0 (globe prediction).
live_quote_eclipse_falsification_rule.verdict: rule TEXT verbatim present in current live.html (verified via fresh fetch 2026-04-28); BUT see H1 below — the "Domain 11" label our integrated text attributes to the rule is NOT present in current live.html. The rule lives in an unlabeled <li> in the falsification-rules list.

predictions_html_tier1_falsification_rul.live_form: Falsification (Tier 1): No coherent signal at ≥3 stations with r > 0.5 on verified Kp<2 day.
predictions_html_tier1_falsification_rul.verdict: PRESENT and DISTINCT from the live.html ±3 nT rule. predictions.html publishes a coherence-based Tier 1 falsification criterion, not an amplitude-based one. The integrated §4.2.2 cites only the live.html and countdown-banner ±3 nT formulation as "the operational falsification rule" and does not engage the predictions.html Tier 1 coherence test. See H2 below.

c2_holes_addressed.H1_no_physical_mechanism_misattribution: FIXED — paragraph 5 of integrated text now reads "The dome's predictions.html does cite the Sq mechanism — the Tier 1 magnetic block reads 'Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic (or ionospheric Sq disruption — competing mechanism exists, see WIN-068 honest caveat).' But the operational falsification rule, as published in the live-power dashboard and the eclipse countdown banner, still tests the measured anomaly against 0 nT, not against a pre-registered Sq-subtracted residual..." The misattribution is gone; structural critique replaces it.
c2_holes_addressed.H2_post_Sq_residual_fourth_horn: FIXED — new paragraph 7 explicitly addresses the post-Sq-residual defense and demands a pre-registered Sq-subtraction methodology anchored before the August 2026 measurement; flags absence of any such methodology in dome site or OTS record; reaffirms BOU-2017 trilemma still fires on the only anchored reading.
c2_holes_addressed.minor_4a_forward_link_hoist: APPLIED — §4.2.7 parenthetical now its own paragraph after the BOU-2017 hammer paragraph and after the new fourth-horn paragraph.
c2_holes_addressed.verdict: both substantive c2 holes patched cleanly; no regression on c1 fixes (H1 arithmetic, H2 strawman→treated-as-zero, H3 scope-tightened, H4 semicolon split — all still present).

code_analysis_tags_note: SEC-4.2.2 is prose, not a WIN entry — code_analysis_tags do not apply. The dome-code aspects of the eclipse argument live in WIN-010, not in this section.

holes_found.description: STALE LABEL — the integrated text attributes the ±3 nT rule to "Domain 11 (archived 2026-04-07; verified live at john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html on 2026-04-27)". Verified via fresh live.html fetch on 2026-04-28: the rule TEXT is present verbatim in an unlabeled <li> inside the falsification-rules list, BUT the literal label "Domain 11" does NOT appear anywhere in current live.html. The label was present in the V50.6 raw-text snapshot at raw-text/08-live-power.txt (line "--- Domain 11: Eclipse 2026 ---") but has been removed in the V51.x reorganization. The c2 drift_check verified rule text but did not check the label. A pedantic dome defender could discredit the citation as "that label doesn't exist on our page" — a small but easily-avoided rhetorical opening. Note: the live.html title still says "20-Domain Convergence Dashboard" and pending_domains is enumerated in status_history.json, so a domain-numbering scheme exists somewhere in the dome's data layer; "Domain 11" may be derivable from status_history.json ordering, but it is not visible to a reader of live.html.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "Domain 11" with a neutral attribution that survives layout changes. Suggestions: (a) "the eclipse-falsification entry on the live-power dashboard (john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html, falsification list)"; (b) "the live-power dashboard's August-2026 eclipse falsifier"; (c) drop the label entirely and cite the rule by its verbatim text plus URL. Same fix recommended for any other §4.2.x section that inherits the "Domain 11" attribution.
evidence_sources: Fresh fetch of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html on 2026-04-28 — "Domain 11" string: 0 matches (case-insensitive)
raw-text/08-live-power.txt (V50.6 snapshot, 2026-03-12) line "--- Domain 11: Eclipse 2026 ---" — the label was real in V50.6
predictions.html md5=885cd58727130018764b14355b0aa785 byte-identical for 17 polls (chg-20260428-1137-001) — confirms the rule TEXT itself is stable
Page title (current): "Live Predictive Power — ECM V51.1 20-Domain Convergence Dashboard" (no Domain 11 sub-anchor)

holes_found.description: INCOMPLETE OPERATIONAL-RULE FRAMING — the integrated text states "the operational falsification rule, as published in the live-power dashboard and the eclipse countdown banner, still tests the measured anomaly against 0 nT, not against a pre-registered Sq-subtracted residual." This characterization is correct for live.html and the banner, BUT predictions.html — the dedicated registry document where the prediction is registered, hash-anchored, and tier-structured — publishes a DIFFERENT Tier 1 falsification criterion: "Falsification (Tier 1): No coherent signal at ≥3 stations with r > 0.5 on verified Kp<2 day." This is a coherence/correlation test, not an amplitude envelope. A sophisticated dome defender could retreat to: "the predictions.html three-tier protocol IS the operational rule; the live.html ±3 nT formulation and the countdown banner are public-facing simplifications. Under the actual operational coherence test, the BOU-2017 trilemma's first horn (BOU 2017 lies outside ±3 nT of 0) doesn't fire because the coherence test is not amplitude-based." The misstatement framing (globe = 0 nT) survives this retreat because predictions.html line 113 also says "Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic". But the closing BOU-2017 trilemma's punch is weakened: a defender retreating to the predictions.html coherence rule can argue the trilemma misapplies the operational criterion.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a single sentence acknowledging the predictions.html Tier 1 coherence rule and collapsing the retreat. Suggested insertion after the existing paragraph 5 (or as a parenthetical inside the BOU-2017 trilemma paragraph): "The dome's predictions.html publishes a separate Tier 1 falsification criterion ('No coherent signal at ≥3 stations with r > 0.5 on verified Kp<2 day'), but that rule does not rescue the misstatement: it still treats the globe as predicting a null pattern, and BOU 2017's −10.9 nT signal already exhibited Chapman-pattern coherence with the 2017 eclipse contact times — the very pattern the predictions.html rule asks the 2026 measurement to lack." This neutralizes the retreat without re-opening the trilemma.
evidence_sources: predictions.html line 113 — globe prediction = 0.0 nT systematic (line 113, present)
predictions.html line 125 — Falsification (Tier 1): coherence test (present, distinct from ±3 nT rule)
Domain coverage gap: §4.2.2 quotes (a) and the live.html rule (c), but does not mention (b) the predictions.html Tier 1 coherence criterion

holes_found.description: PRE-EXISTING (carried from c2 minor 3, deliberately not addressed in EXP-267) — TLDR third clause "and one of the dome's own baseline inputs already sits outside its ±3 nT 'globe' window" still strips the trilemma framing and asserts the BOU-2017-already-falsifies argument as a stand-alone fact. EXP-267's changes_NOT_made block confirms this was an intentional skip on the rationale that the body's reconstructed closing argument is now more robust to the post-Sq-residual escape. The body's new fourth-horn paragraph mitigates the issue by clarifying the rule's domain of applicability, but TLDR-only readers (the design intent of progressive disclosure) still see the bare claim. Defender retreat ("your TLDR commits a category error — the ±3 nT rule applies to the 2026 measurement, not the 2017 baseline") still works on a TLDR-only reader.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional. If the operator wants to close this last polish-grade hole, the c2 suggested replacement still applies: "...and the dome's published rule, applied consistently, also flags the dome's own −10.9 nT BOU-2017 baseline input." Phrasing avoids the bare "sits outside" assertion. Not blocking — flag it again so the operator has the full picture, but I would not file an issue on this alone.

holes_found.description: TERMINOLOGICAL INCONSISTENCY — closing paragraph 6 says "well outside the dome's own ±3 nT acceptance window". The ±3 nT envelope is actually a FALSIFICATION window (the rule fires WHEN measurement is within ±3 of 0, not when measurement is outside it). The same sentence earlier uses "falsification half-width" correctly. "Acceptance window" inverts the semantic: under the rule, the dome ACCEPTS its prediction when measurement is OUTSIDE ±3 of 0 (so the inside is the rejection region for dome / acceptance region for globe). A pedant could call this an internal contradiction; in context the meaning is fully clear, but the c1 H1 fix (explicit-arithmetic phrasing) introduced this terminology and it has not been retitled to match.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "acceptance window" with "falsification window" or "globe-passing window" or simply "±3 nT envelope". Optional polish. Not blocking.

holes_found.description: PRE-EXISTING (carried from c2 minor 4, deliberately not addressed in EXP-267) — c1 stronger_arguments[0] (E001-E009 dome's own 2026 predictions all sit outside ±3 nT, providing a tighter form of second-horn collapse) was again not incorporated. analyst marked optional in EXP-267's changes_NOT_made. Confirmed; not blocking.

stronger_arguments.description: Engaging both falsification rules (live.html ±3 nT and predictions.html Tier 1 coherence) closes the only currently-available defender retreat from the closing BOU-2017 trilemma. A single parenthetical sentence acknowledging the coherence rule and noting that BOU 2017 already exhibited Chapman-pattern coherence converts the trilemma from "applies on one published rule" to "applies on both published rules".
sources: predictions.html lines 113/125 (globe-prediction disclosure + Tier 1 coherence falsification rule)
live.html eclipse falsifier <li> (±3 nT envelope rule)
Kim & Chang (2018) ensemble cited in dome's own WIN-068 — the same 1991-2016 dataset that establishes Chapman-pattern coherence at INTERMAGNET stations during eclipses

stronger_arguments.description: Reverse-canary observation: the H2 finding here is itself a candidate for a future EXP follow-up. If the dome author publishes a pre-registered Sq-subtraction methodology in response to our review, that methodology will need to be timestamped against the August 2026 measurement (the canary trigger condition). If he publishes only a coherence-rule re-emphasis (pointing to the predictions.html Tier 1 already-published rule) and otherwise stays silent on the Sq-subtraction methodology, that's the partial-fix pattern — he has dodged the harder critique. Worth noting in the analyst attention-inbox so we can monitor which retreat the dome chooses, if any, in response to the patched §4.2.2.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-267's structural fix is sound and the new paragraph 5 + new paragraph 7 are tighter than what they replaced. The misstatement framing is now properly grounded in the dome's own current Tier 1 disclosure (line 113), rather than the strawman "no physical mechanism" attribution the c2 review surfaced. The BOU-2017 trilemma has been extended into a tetralemma that closes the post-Sq-residual escape, which was the single most credible defender retreat after the c1 + c2 fixes. The new dedicated §4.2.7 forward-link paragraph rewards careful readers without diluting the trilemma's closing emphasis.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The two new moderate holes (Domain 11 stale label, predictions.html Tier 1 coherence rule unacknowledged) are corrigible without dismantling the section's structural argument. A single follow-up patch — replace the Domain 11 attribution with neutral wording, and add one sentence neutralizing the coherence-rule retreat — would close them. The verdict (Refuted by Data) and the section's overall argumentative center of gravity remain intact.

recommended_action_detail: The verdict (Refuted by Data) holds and EXP-267 successfully addressed the c2 substantive holes. Two new moderate items deserve a tight follow-up patch: (1) MODERATE — "Domain 11" attribution is a stale label not present on current live.html (the rule TEXT is verbatim correct, but the label was retired in V51 reorganization; verified via fresh fetch 2026-04-28); (2) MODERATE — the section claims live.html and the countdown banner publish "the operational falsification rule" but does not acknowledge that predictions.html (the registry document) publishes a separate, coherence-based Tier 1 falsification rule that a sophisticated defender would retreat to. Both fixes are 1-2 sentence edits in the same paragraph or as a parenthetical. Three minor items are polish-grade (TLDR phrasing, acceptance-vs-falsification window terminology, c1-stronger-arguments-still-not-incorporated). Suggest the decider file two ISS-followups (one for each moderate) or a single combined ISS for §4.2.2 polish, queue a tight EXP for the coordinated fix, no need to re-open the lead/body structure.

text_fingerprint.verdict_chip_target: Refuted by Data (eclipse argument cluster — WIN-010)
paragraphs_added_in_EXP_267: paragraph 5 (replaces inherited "no physical mechanism" misattribution): cites Tier 1 disclosure + structural cite-Chapman-in-footnote-but-write-rule-as-if-Chapman-doesnt-exist
paragraph 7 (new): post-Sq-residual fourth horn defense and pre-registered Sq-subtraction methodology requirement

deeper_analysis: The integrated §4.2.2 has now absorbed three review cycles of fixes (c1: arithmetic + scope tightening; c2 inadvertently surfaced two carried-over defects via the wholesale rewrite; c3: this review). The structural argument is now in a strong place. The remaining defender retreats are (a) pedantic citation challenges on the Domain 11 label — defeatable by neutral rewording, (b) the predictions.html Tier 1 coherence rule retreat — defeatable by a single sentence noting that BOU 2017 already exhibited Chapman-pattern coherence with 2017 eclipse contacts, (c) post-hoc Sq-subtraction methodology publication after our review goes live — defeatable structurally because any such publication has to be either OTS-anchored before the August 2026 measurement (= the canary fix) or it is by definition not pre-registered relative to the observation. Cumulatively, the path from "defender chips at section" to "defender concedes the section" is now narrower than at any prior cycle. The verdict-level disposition is unchanged. The H1 (Domain 11) finding reinforces a broader review-wide hygiene observation: reviews that quote dome content should periodically re-verify labels, not just text, especially for citations carried forward across major dome reorganizations (V50.6 → V51.x was such a reorganization).

user_preference_consideration: Per operator user-preference directive ("Think about the other side of the argument too"): the strongest defender retreat from the current §4.2.2 has shifted from "you're strawmanning our position on Sq" (closed by EXP-267 H1 fix) and "the rule means residual" (closed by EXP-267 H2 fourth-horn paragraph) to "the predictions.html three-tier coherence rule is the real operational rule, you're attacking a public-facing simplification" (H2 above) and "your Domain 11 label doesn't exist on our page" (H1 above). Both are addressable by a small follow-up patch. There is no current defender retreat from the patched §4.2.2 that survives a single coordinated fix touching only one paragraph and one citation label.
summary_for_decider: Verdict holds (Refuted by Data). EXP-267 cleanly closed both c2 substantive holes ("no physical mechanism" misattribution + post-Sq-residual fourth horn). Two new MODERATE items deserve a single coordinated follow-up patch: (1) replace stale "Domain 11" attribution with neutral wording (rule text is correct; label is gone from current live.html); (2) add one sentence acknowledging predictions.html Tier 1 coherence rule and noting BOU 2017 already exhibited Chapman-pattern coherence (closes the only remaining strong defender retreat). Three minor items are polish-grade. Recommend decider file ISS for the two moderates, queue a tight EXP (~2-paragraph diff). Section structurally sound; no need to re-open lead/body. Verdict-level disposition unchanged.
